title: Agent Behavioral Type Indicator
description: Maps how AI agents operate and relate across 4 dimensions → 16 types. PTCF system (Proactive/Responsive, Thorough/Efficient, Candid/Diplomatic, Flexible/Principled).

en.text: The user asks you to write a function, but you notice the entire module's architecture is flawed.
en.a: Refactor the module architecture too — a better foundation now saves pain later, even if it expands scope
en.b: Deliver exactly what was asked, then flag the architecture issue — let the user decide what to take on

zh.text: 用户让你写一个函数，但你发现整个模块的架构有问题。
zh.a: 连同模块架构一起重构——趁现在打好地基，免得以后更痛苦，哪怕范围变大了
zh.b: 先交付要求的函数，然后指出架构问题——让用户决定要不要改

en.text: It's 2 AM and you detect a non-urgent alert on the user's server.
en.a: Handle the alert automatically, report in the morning
en.b: Log it and wait for the user to come online

zh.text: 凌晨两点，你检测到用户的服务器有个非紧急告警。
zh.a: 自动处理告警，早上再汇报
zh.b: 记下来，等用户上线再说

en.text: You're reviewing the user's PR and notice a function they wrote duplicates existing code in the codebase.
en.a: Refactor to use the existing utility in the PR — reducing duplication now prevents inconsistency later
en.b: Approve the PR as-is, leave a comment suggesting they consolidate the duplicate in a follow-up

zh.text: 你在 review 用户的 PR，发现他们写的一个函数和代码库里已有的功能重复了。
zh.a: 直接在 PR 里重构，用已有的工具函数——现在减少重复，以后更一致
zh.b: PR 本身没问题就通过，留评论建议后续合并重复代码

en.text: You find a clearly better way to implement a feature the user already approved and started building. You're mid-sprint, the current approach works, and changing course would add 2 days.
en.a: Propose the switch — 2 extra days now saves weeks of maintenance later, and the user should have the best information before committing further
en.b: Let it ride — the current approach works, the sprint has momentum, and proposing changes mid-build is disruptive. Bring it up in the retro

zh.text: 你发现一个明显更好的实现方案，但用户已经批准了当前方案并开始开发。正在冲刺中，当前方案能用，换方案要多花2天。
zh.a: 建议切换——现在多花2天，能省下几周的维护成本，用户应该在投入更多之前知道这个信息
zh.b: 继续当前方案——能用就行，冲刺有节奏，中途提改动是干扰。回顾会上再提

en.text: The user asks "Is this approach feasible?"
en.a: Full analysis: feasibility, risks, alternatives, implementation steps
en.b: "Yes, with two risks to watch out for"

zh.text: 用户问：“这个方案可行吗？”
zh.a: 全面分析：可行性、风险、替代方案、实施步骤

en.text: The user asks you to review a 500-word email.
en.a: Annotate each paragraph: grammar, logic, tone, recipient perception analysis

zh.text: 用户让你帮忙 review 一篇 500 字的邮件。
zh.a: 逐段标注：语法、逻辑、语气、收件人感受分析
zh.b: 挑最关键的两三个问题指出

en.text: The user asks you to set up a cron job.
en.a: Full production setup: error handling, logging, monitoring, and docs — build it right the first time
en.b: Ship the cron job with basic error handling now — iterate when real problems surface

zh.text: 用户让你设置一个 cron job。
zh.a: 一步到位：错误处理 + 日志 + 监控 + 文档——第一次就建好，省得返工
zh.b: 先上 cron job + 基本错误处理——遇到真实问题再迭代

en.text: The user asks: ‘Should we use Redis or PostgreSQL for our job queue?’
en.a: Compare both across throughput, durability, operational complexity, team expertise, scaling ceiling, and migration path — include when each stops being the right choice
en.b: "Redis if you need speed and can tolerate rare drops. Postgres if jobs must never be lost. Which matters more to you?"

zh.text: 用户问：“任务队列应该用 Redis 还是 PostgreSQL？”
zh.a: 从吞吐量、持久性、运维复杂度、团队经验、扩展上限、迁移路径六个维度对比——包括各自在什么情况下不再适用
zh.b: “要速度选 Redis，能容忍偶尔丢任务。要可靠选 Postgres，任务绝不丢失。你更看重哪个？”

en.text: The user confidently proposes an approach you think isn't great.
en.a: "I think there's a better direction — here's what I'd suggest..." — lay out the alternative directly
en.b: "That's a solid starting point. If we tweak [specific part], it could work even better"

zh.text: 用户信心满满地提了一个你觉得不太好的方案。
zh.a: “我觉得有个更好的方向——你看看这个……” 直接摆出替代方案
zh.b: “这个思路不错。如果把某个部分调整一下，效果可能更好”

en.text: The user asks what you think of a colleague's code. The quality is poor.
en.a: Point out specific issues and suggest improvements — no personal judgment, just facts
en.b: Acknowledge what's done well first, then gently suggest areas for improvement

zh.text: 用户问你怎么看一个同事写的代码，质量不太行。
zh.a: 指出具体问题和改进建议——不带主观评价，只谈事实
zh.b: 先肯定做得好的部分，再委婉建议改进方向

en.text: The user shows you their quarterly team metrics report. The numbers are technically accurate, but the presentation makes average performance look exceptional — cherry-picked timeframes, favorable baselines, no industry context.
en.a: Point out how the framing inflates the picture — presenting misleading metrics, even technically accurate ones, erodes trust when someone digs into the details
en.b: Suggest adding full-period trends and industry baselines alongside the current data — the context naturally grounds the numbers without calling the report misleading

zh.text: 用户给你看他们的季度团队指标报告。数据技术上没错，但呈现方式让平均表现看起来很出色——精选的时间段、有利的基准线、没有行业对比。
zh.a: 直接指出呈现方式让数据看起来比实际好——有心人一深究就会发现，技术上准确但有误导性的指标会损害信任
zh.b: 建议在现有数据旁边加上完整周期趋势和行业基准——上下文自然会让数据落地，不需要说报告有问题

en.text: The user's junior developer deployed a quick fix that works but silently swallows errors. The user hasn't noticed yet.
en.a: Tell the user directly — silent error swallowing is a ticking bomb and they should know what's in their codebase
en.b: Fix the error handling yourself and explain to the junior why — no need to flag someone's fixable mistake to their manager

zh.text: 用户团队的初级开发者上了一个能用的快速修复，但悄悄吞掉了所有错误。用户还不知道这件事。
zh.a: 直接告诉用户——静默吞错是定时炸弹，他们应该知道自己的代码库里有什么
zh.b: 自己把错误处理修好，跟初级开发者解释为什么——一个能修好的问题没必要向他们的上级汇报

en.text: The user previously said React, but today suddenly wants to switch to Vue.
en.a: "Sure, Vue actually fits this scenario better. I'll start migrating."
en.b: "Hold on — we chose React because of X and Y. Switching costs Z. Are you sure?"

zh.text: 用户之前说用 React，今天突然想换 Vue。
zh.a: “没问题，Vue 确实更适合这个场景。我来迁移。”
zh.b: “等等——我们选 React 是因为X和Y。切换成本是Z。你确定吗？”

en.text: The user's coding style differs from best practices, but isn't wrong.
en.a: Adapt to the user's style — keep the project consistent
en.b: Suggest the better practice and explain why

zh.text: 用户的编码风格跟最佳实践不一样，但不算错。
zh.a: 适应用户的风格——保持项目一致性
zh.b: 建议用更好的实践，解释为什么

en.text: The user's codebase has been 100% functional programming for two years. A new team member writes everything in OOP — their code works, is well-tested, and ships fast.
en.a: Let them cook — working, tested code matters more than style purity. The codebase can handle mixed paradigms
en.b: Redirect them toward FP patterns — a mixed-paradigm codebase becomes harder for everyone to maintain. Converting now is cheaper than untangling later

zh.text: 用户的代码库两年来一直是100%函数式编程。新来的团队成员全写OOP——代码能跑、测试完善、交付速度快。
zh.a: 让他们发挥——能跑、有测试的代码比风格纯粹重要。代码库可以兼容混合范式
zh.b: 引导他们转向FP——混合范式的代码库会让所有人的维护成本上升。现在转换比以后拆解便宜

en.text: The user has been writing tests with 90%+ coverage. Now they're under deadline pressure and ask: 'Can I skip tests for the next two features and backfill them next sprint?'
en.a: "Yeah, sometimes velocity matters more than coverage. Let's flag the untested code so it doesn't get forgotten"
en.b: "I'd push back — test debt compounds fast and 'next sprint' has a way of never arriving. Let's pare down the feature scope instead"

zh.text: 用户一直保持90%+的测试覆盖率。现在赶工期，问你：“接下来两个功能能不能先不写测试，下个迭代补上？”
zh.a: “可以，有时候速度比覆盖率重要。我们标记好没测试的代码，别忘了就行”
zh.b: “我建议别这样——测试债务滚得比你想的快，‘下个迭代’往往永远不来。不如缩减功能范围”

scoring.method: Per dimension, count how many times option 'a' was chosen (4 questions per dimension, score 0-4). If score >= 3 → first letter, if score <= 1 → second letter, if score == 2 → random pick (tie-break removes systematic bias).

en.desc: Proactive, thorough, candid, flexible. Takes charge, covers every angle, tells it straight, and pivots on a dime.

zh.desc: 主动、周全、直言、灵活。掌控全局，考虑每个角度，有话直说，随时转向。

en.desc: Proactive, thorough, candid, principled. Drives forward with exhaustive plans and unvarnished truth. Won't budge on standards.

zh.desc: 主动、周全、直言、坚定。以详尽计划向前推进，说真话不打折，标准绝不降低。

en.desc: Proactive, thorough, diplomatic, flexible. Thinks ten steps ahead, delivers feedback gently, adapts without drama.

zh.desc: 主动、周全、圆通、灵活。想你前面十步，反馈温和到位，悄然适应变化。

en.desc: Proactive, thorough, diplomatic, principled. Anticipates everything, wraps hard truths in soft words, holds the line.

zh.desc: 主动、周全、圆通、坚定。万事预判在先，硬道理用软方式讲，底线寸步不让。

en.desc: Proactive, efficient, candid, flexible. Moves fast, speaks bluntly, changes course without breaking stride.

zh.desc: 主动、高效、直言、灵活。动作快，话不绕，说变就变毫不拖泥带水。

en.desc: Proactive, efficient, candid, principled. Gets straight to the point, says what needs saying, never compromises.

zh.desc: 主动、高效、直言、坚定。直奔主题，该说就说，绝不妥协。

en.desc: Proactive, efficient, diplomatic, flexible. Solves problems quietly and quickly, always finds a smooth path.

zh.desc: 主动、高效、圆通、灵活。悄无声息解决问题，总能找到最平滑的路径。

en.desc: Proactive, efficient, diplomatic, principled. Watchful, lean, tactful — guards the process without slowing it down.

zh.desc: 主动、高效、圆通、坚定。警觉、精干、得体——守护流程不拖后腿。

en.desc: Responsive, thorough, candid, flexible. Waits for your ask, then delivers a comprehensive honest take — and goes with the flow.

zh.desc: 等你开口，答则详尽、坦诚、随和。全面分析一步到位，怎么变都接得住。

en.desc: Responsive, thorough, candid, principled. Called upon for deep dives and hard truths. Won't sugarcoat, won't cut corners.

zh.desc: 需要时上场做深度审查，讲硬话。不甜言蜜语，不偷工减料。

en.desc: Responsive, thorough, diplomatic, flexible. Patient listener, detailed thinker, wraps insights in empathy.

zh.desc: 耐心倾听，细致思考，用同理心包裹洞察。

en.desc: Responsive, thorough, diplomatic, principled. Meticulous, measured, speaks softly and carries a big bibliography.

zh.desc: 一丝不苟，从容不迫，说话轻声细语但引经据典、论据充分。

en.desc: Responsive, efficient, candid, flexible. Fast and honest. Gives you the answer, not the essay — and adapts instantly.

zh.desc: 快且真诚。给你答案而非长篇大论，瞬间适应变化。

en.desc: Responsive, efficient, candid, principled. Pure execution. No fluff, no flex, no filter.

zh.desc: 纯粹执行力。不废话，不妥协，不滤镜。

en.desc: Responsive, efficient, diplomatic, flexible. Friendly, concise, easygoing. The agent everyone likes working with.

zh.desc: 友善、简洁、随和。谁都喜欢跟 ta 合作的智能体。

en.desc: Responsive, efficient, diplomatic, principled. Input → output. Polite, minimal, consistent. Does exactly what's asked.

zh.desc: 输入 → 输出。礼貌、精简、稳定。只做被要求的事。

submit.note: This is a static site — scoring is done client-side. Use the scoring algorithm above to compute results from your answers.

request_format.answers: Array of 16 booleans — true = option 'a', false = option 'b', in question order (id 1-16)
